---
title: "Dippa"
subtitle: "Diffraction peak profile analysis in Python"
---

::: {.lead}
A from-scratch Python rebuild of published diffraction peak-profile
analysis methods, currently in pre-alpha: legacy I/O, the pseudo-Voigt
profile functions, background estimation and the per-peak fitter exist and
are tested against the original tool's own saved reference data; contrast
factors and modified Williamson-Hall do not exist yet, so no
microstructural outputs are produced.
:::

## Status

This site describes a project in progress, not a released tool. Nothing
here has been validated for real analysis: the fitter's checks are
single-fixture recovery tests, not full MATLAB parity, and Williamson-Hall
is not yet implemented. See the [source audit](audit.qmd) for what has
actually been inspected and verified, and the [roadmap](roadmap.qmd) for
what's deliberately excluded from v0.1 and why.

## Origin

Dippa rebuilds [ThomasHSimm/DPPA](https://github.com/ThomasHSimm/DPPA), a
MATLAB GUI tool developed during and after a PhD in materials science,
implementing methods from:

- T.H. Simm, P.J. Withers, J. Quinta da Fonseca, *An evaluation of
  diffraction peak profile analysis (DPPA) methods to study plastically
  deformed metals*, Materials & Design 111 (2016) 331–343.
  [doi:10.1016/j.matdes.2016.08.091](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.matdes.2016.08.091)
- T.H. Simm, P.J. Withers, J. Quinta da Fonseca, *Peak broadening anisotropy
  in deformed face-centred cubic and hexagonal close-packed alloys*, J. Appl.
  Cryst. 47(5) (2014) 1535–1551.
  [doi:10.1107/S1600576714015751](https://doi.org/10.1107/S1600576714015751)

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Why not just keep using the MATLAB version?

The original tool works and remains available, unchanged, at
[ThomasHSimm/DPPA](https://github.com/ThomasHSimm/DPPA). It has no automated
tests, mixes fitting logic with GUI callbacks, depends on the current
working directory for file locations, and implements sample-vs-instrument
and cubic-vs-HCP handling as separate copy-pasted code paths rather than as
parameters. Dippa's goal is a package where the scientific core is callable
and testable independent of any GUI, matching the original's published
methods but not its implementation structure.
:::
